highlights:

### Senior AI Engineer at Sherwin-Williams (Current)
...
Feb 2025 - Present (1 year and 4 months) in Ohio, United States
...
Designed and developed enterprise-grade Generative AI and Agentic AI solutions to automate document processing, information extraction, and workflow orchestration using Large Language Models (LLMs). Built multi-agent systems using LangChain and LangGraph for task decomposition, reasoning, and autonomous decision-making across enterprise workflows. Implemented end-to-end Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipelines including document ingestion, chunking, embedding generation, metadata extraction, and vector indexing using Pinecone and ChromaDB. Developed scalable backend services using FastAPI to expose AI capabilities as production-ready APIs for enterprise applications. Designed NLP pipelines for text classification, summarization, and semantic search. Implemented prompt engineering strategies, evaluation frameworks, and feedback loops to improve response accuracy and reduce hallucinations in production systems. Deployed containerized applications on AWS using Docker and EC2, and implemented MLflow for experiment tracking and model lifecycle management.
...
### Machine Learning Engineer at Oracle
...
23 (3 years
...
) in Bengaluru, Karnataka, India
...
Developed and deployed machine learning and NLP solutions for enterprise-scale applications using Python and cloud platforms. Built NLP systems using HuggingFace Transformers for text classification, named entity recognition, and summarization tasks. Developed predictive models using Scikit-learn, Random Forest, and XGBoost for forecasting and analytics use cases. Designed and implemented ETL pipelines using Python and SQL for large-scale structured and unstructured data processing. Performed data preprocessing, feature engineering, and model training using Pandas and NumPy. Optimized model performance through hyperparameter tuning techniques such as grid search and cross-validation, improving accuracy and generalization. Developed and deployed REST APIs using Flask to serve ML models for real-time predictions. Worked on improving inference performance and reducing latency in production ML systems. Containerized applications using Docker and supported deployments on GCP environments.

results.title: Nico Lindsey
highlights:
Senior Manager of AI MLOps at [Sherwin-Williams](https://www.linkedin.com/company/sherwin-williams)
...
Hi, I’m Nico! Welcome to my profile. As a visionary AI/ML and software engineering leader, I have consistently delivered transformative solutions that drive business growth and optimize processes. With a strong background in leveraging cutting-edge technologies such as AI, ML, cloud, agile, and DevOps, I empower organizations to achieve their ambitious goals. ➥My expertise lies in building and leading high-performing, diverse teams that push the boundaries of innovation. By fostering a culture of collaboration and continuous learning, I inspire my teams to develop groundbreaking solutions that address complex business challenges. ➥Throughout my career, I have spearheaded numerous high-impact projects, including the development of an Alternative Investment Platform that processed over $1 billion in private market investments, the creation of a highly scalable cloud-hosted microservice architecture, and the automation of insurance claims processing using advanced machine learning models. ➥I am passionate about aligning technology initiatives with organizational objectives and collaborating with stakeholders to drive continuous improvement. With a strategic mindset and a deep understanding of the latest technological advancements, I am committed to helping businesses stay ahead of the curve in today's rapidly evolving digital landscape. If you are seeking a visionary leader who can transform your organization through the power of AI, ML, and software engineering, let's connect and explore how I can help you achieve your goals.

results.title: Dan Metzendorf
highlights:
Senior Director Business & Insights at The Sherwin-Williams Company
...
Director Data Science at The Sherwin-Williams Company
...
I help business decision makers make better decisions. In order to make sense of the large volume of data that can inform the modern business decision maker and create some actionable insight from it, I leverage my IT and statistical skills along with my ability to form a strong partnership with business leaders. I have experience with strategic supply chain optimization, product pricing strategy, as well as customer insight projects. My career thus far has involved everything from technical programming to managing complex IT application implementations, and it is my experience that each Data Science engagement affords me the opportunity to utilize my varied skill set. With each Data Science project, it is unknown where each step in the process may lead you in search of insights, but the ability to think on your feet, and adapt to what the data AND your business partner is telling you is absolutely key. I cannot and will never claim to know every possible analysis technique applicable to every possible business area. My job is to jump in and help business leaders understand what is going on, act as a buffer between those business people and the complexity of the data, and work as hard as I can to drive towards the insights and answers that data contains. If I need to acquire new techniques in order to accomplish that goal, then bring it on. Send an e-mail to Daniel.Metzendorf@gmail.com to learn more. Specialties: Collaboration, Innovation, R, Data Science, SQL
...

📉 Advanced Graph Analytics with Neo4j Exploring the power of Neo4j to unlock relationship-driven intelligence.

    ✔ Knowledge Graph
    ✔ Community Detection
    ✔ PageRank & Centrality Analysis
    ✔ Shortest Path Optimization
    ✔ Similarity Scoring
    ✔ Anomaly Detection
    ✔ Graph RAG
...
🪻Reference:
    ✨ Cypher + Graph: https://lnkd.in/gcdKPbKd ✨ GraphRAG: https://lnkd.in/gBesDe5y ✨ GraphAI: https://lnkd.in/gtaDGtzt Neo4j Artificial Intelligence Python #Neo4j #GraphAnalytics #AI #GraphRAG #
...
Engineering #KnowledgeGraph #Python
...
Right now, most of the open Data Scientist jobs are about working with LLMs.
...
But here’s the catch: only a small percentage of real business problems can be solved with them. 

The truth is:
...
▪️The majority of business challenges still require classical machine learning, statistics, optimization, and causal inference.
...
▪️ For tabular data and time series, they are almost useless, and those represent the biggest part of the data in many industries.
...
▪️Problems like forecasting, anomaly detection, demand planning, fraud detection, recommendation, predictive maintenance, etc, are rarely best solved by an LLM.
...
▪️Deploying LLMs where they don’t fit often leads to overengineering, high costs, and disappointing ROI.
...
Yet somehow, the market acts like LLMs are the silver bullet for everything. 
Spoiler: they’re not.
...
Most companies will ultimately waste money on GPUs to solve problems that regression models could address for a fraction of
...
LLMs are impressive, but they’re also a hype cycle, not the foundation of data science.
...
And the best data scientists know: it’s not about the fanciest model, it’s about solving the right problem with the right tool.
